---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_15.png){.title-icon} Learning resources"
subtitle: "Courses, books and references for R, epidemiology and statistics"
date: last-modified
---

**If you only pick one:** Start with [DDEA Introductory
Course](https://r-cubed-intro.rostools.org/) if you are completely new to R, or
[The Epidemiologist R Handbook](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/) if you know a
bit of R and want to go straight to health science applications.

## Getting started - learn R

  | Resource                                                           | What it is                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
  | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | ⭐ [DDEA Introductory Course](https://r-cubed-intro.rostools.org/) | Introductory R course from the Danish Diabetes and Endocrine Academy - held once a year, materials freely available. Best starting point if you are completely new.                                                                                                          |
  | ⭐ [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)                   | Free e-book by Hadley Wickham - the standard reference for modern R with tidyverse. Concept-based, read from start to finish.                                                                                                                                                |
  | [Cookbook for R](http://www.cookbook-r.com)                        | Task-based reference: "how do I do X?" with direct code examples. Strongest for its ggplot2 graphics recipes (legends, faceting, colours). Note: parts of the base-R and data-handling sections predate the tidyverse, so the syntax can differ from the rest of this guide. |
  | [Quick-R](https://www.statmethods.net)                             | Compact reference page for R syntax and statistics. Good for quick lookups mid-script.                                                                                                                                                                                       |

## Health science and epidemiology

  | Resource                                                                      | What it is                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
  | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | ⭐ [The Epidemiologist R Handbook](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/)          | Practical reference written for epidemiologists and health researchers - covers data cleaning, analyses and visualisation with health science examples. Most relevant chapters for register work: dates, joins, data cleaning, regression and survival analysis. Most pages in this guide link to the matching chapter. |
  | ⭐ [Zheers R Coding Café](https://r-coding-cafe.zheer.dk/)                    | Notes and examples specifically for register data - local to Steno Diabetes Center Aarhus.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
  | [CRAN Task View: Epidemiology](https://cran.r-project.org/view=Epidemiology)  | Package list for epidemiology - incidence rates, matching, cohort analysis. Good starting point when looking for the right package.                                                                                                                                                                                     |
  | [CRAN Task View: Survival Analysis](https://cran.r-project.org/view=Survival) | Package list for survival analysis - Cox regression, Kaplan–Meier and competing risks.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

**Stuck?** Look up common problems in [Common
errors](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/new_pages/errors.html), and learn how to
ask a good question in [Getting
help](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/new_pages/help.html) (both in The
Epidemiologist R Handbook). On DST you cannot share your confidential data when
asking for help - simulate a small, fake dataset that reproduces the problem
instead. [Minimal Reproducible
Example](https://r-coding-cafe.zheer.dk/notes/lesson-notes/01-Minimal-Reproducible-Example.html)
(Zheer's R Coding Café) shows how.

**Podcasts:**

  | Resource                                                                    | What it is                                                                                                                            |
  | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | 🎧 [SERious Epi](https://seriousepi.blubrry.net/)                           | Podcast on epidemiological methods in practice - study design, bias and analytical choices, discussed by experienced epidemiologists. |
  | 🎧 [Causal Inference](https://open.spotify.com/show/1L8TqB17Peo7jNgXuPObwi) | Podcast on causal inference - methods, confounding and causal questions, in an informal conversational format.                        |

## Methods and statistics

  | Resource                                                                           | What it is                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
  | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | [Learning Statistics with R](https://learningstatisticswithr.com/)                 | Free e-book by Danielle Navarro - covers statistical theory and R implementation side by side, from basic probability to regression and ANOVA. Written for a non-mathematical audience. Relevant if you want to understand the statistics you are running, not just copy the code. |
  | [Causal Inference: What If (Hernán & Robins)](https://miguelhernan.org/whatifbook) | Free PDF - the standard reference on causal inference, confounding and DAGs. Epidemiological methods, not R.                                                                                                                                                                       |
